entities.description: Practices used to prevent soil erosion and maintain soil fertility

entities.description: The sequence and spatial arrangement of crops grown on a piece of land

entities.description: The process of collecting and storing rainwater for later use

entities.description: Small barriers constructed across a stream to slow water flow and recharge groundwater

entities.description: Structures that function as both a road crossing and a water harvesting barrier

entities.name: PERCOLATION TANKS
entities.description: Artificial ponds designed to facilitate the seepage of water into the ground

entities.name: FARM PONDS
entities.description: Small man-made ponds on farms for storing water

entities.name: EARTHEN BUNDS
entities.description: Embankments made of soil to prevent runoff and conserve water

entities.name: WATER STORAGE CAPACITY
entities.description: The total volume of water that can be held within the landscape

entities.name: WATER AVAILABILITY
entities.description: The presence and accessibility of water for agricultural use

entities.name: IRRIGATED AREA
entities.description: The extent of land receiving artificial water supply for crops

entities.name: CROPPING INTENSITY
entities.description: The number of times a crop is planted and harvested on the same land in a year

entities.name: TRADITIONAL CROPS
entities.description: Crops that have been historically grown in the region

entities.name: COMMERCIAL CASH CROPS
entities.description: Crops grown primarily for sale in the market for profit

entities.description: A holistic strategy for managing land and water resources in a watershed

entities.name: KEYSTONE ELEMENTS
entities.description: Critical landscape components that provide multiple essential functions
